questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other cars are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light means you must come to a complete stop and wait until the light turns green before proceeding.

questions.prompt: What is the shape of a stop sign?
choices: Octagon (eight sides)
Triangle
Diamond
Rectangle
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always an eight-sided red octagon, so it can be recognized even from the back or in poor visibility.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light means you should:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop completely before proceeding
Stop and wait for a green light
Continue at your normal speed
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light warns you to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light indicate?
choices: The signal is about to turn red
The signal is about to turn green
You may speed up to beat the light
The signal is broken
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that the signal is changing to red, so you should prepare to stop if you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: What color and shape signs are used for warning of hazards ahead?
choices: Yellow diamond-shaped signs
Red octagonal signs
Green rectangular signs
Blue square signs
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond-shaped signs warn drivers of upcoming hazards or changes in road conditions.

questions.prompt: When a traffic signal is dark and not working at all, you should:
choices: Treat the intersection as a four-way stop
Drive through without stopping
Wait for the light to come back on
Yield only to vehicles on your left
questions.explanation: When signals are completely out, every driver must stop and proceed in turn as if at a four-way stop.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of a two-lane road means:
choices: Passing is not allowed on your side
Passing is always allowed
The road is one-way
You must stop ahead
questions.explanation: A solid yellow center line means passing is prohibited for traffic on that side of the road.

questions.prompt: What does a downward-pointing triangular sign mean?
choices: Yield
Stop
No entry
Construction ahead
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, telling you to slow down and give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: When a police officer's directions conflict with a traffic signal, you should:
choices: Obey the police officer
Obey the traffic signal
Stop and wait for another officer
Choose whichever is safer for you
questions.explanation: You must always follow a police officer's directions, even when they differ from what a signal or sign indicates.

questions.prompt: Orange traffic signs and cones generally indicate:
choices: A construction or maintenance zone
A hospital nearby
A school crossing
A scenic route
questions.explanation: Orange signs and markings warn drivers of construction or maintenance work and require extra caution.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop where two vehicles arrive at the same time, who has the right-of-way?
choices: The driver on the right
The driver on the left
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop together, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When turning left at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians
Only vehicles behind you
No one if you signal first
Only large trucks
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must wait for oncoming traffic and pedestrians to clear before completing the turn.

questions.prompt: In Rhode Island, a right turn on a red light is:
choices: Allowed after a full stop unless a sign prohibits it
Never allowed
Allowed without stopping
Allowed only at night
questions.explanation: You may turn right on red after stopping completely and yielding, unless a posted sign forbids the turn.

questions.prompt: When you approach a crosswalk where a pedestrian is crossing, you must:
choices: Yield and let the pedestrian cross
Honk and continue
Proceed if you have a green light
Speed up to pass before they reach you
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield the right-of-way to pedestrians in marked or unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on, you should:
choices: Pull to the right and stop until it passes
Speed up to get out of the way
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue driving normally
questions.explanation: You must pull over to the right edge of the road and stop to let emergency vehicles pass.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection with no signs or signals, you should:
choices: Yield to the vehicle that arrived first or is on your right
Always go first
Stop and wait one full minute
Yield only to larger vehicles
questions.explanation: At uncontrolled intersections, yield to whoever arrived first, and if simultaneous, to the vehicle on your right.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout or traffic circle, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the circle
Stop completely before entering
Enter first because you have priority
Honk to alert others
questions.explanation: Vehicles already traveling in a roundabout have the right-of-way over those entering it.

questions.prompt: Before making any turn, you should signal:
choices: Early, so others can anticipate your move
Only if other cars are nearby
As you begin the turn
After you complete the turn
questions.explanation: Signaling well before a turn gives other drivers and pedestrians time to react to your intentions.

questions.prompt: When merging onto a highway, you should:
choices: Match the speed of traffic and merge into a gap
Stop at the end of the ramp and wait
Drive slowly until traffic clears
Force your way into the nearest lane
questions.explanation: Merging safely means accelerating to the speed of highway traffic and blending into an available gap.

questions.prompt: A driver entering a road from a driveway or alley must:
choices: Yield to traffic and pedestrians already on the road
Have the right-of-way over road traffic
Stop only if a car is visible
Sound the horn and proceed
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering a roadway from a private driveway or alley must yield to all traffic and pedestrians already there.

questions.prompt: What is the default speed limit in residential and urban districts in Rhode Island?
choices: 25 mph
35 mph
45 mph
55 mph
questions.explanation: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in Rhode Island residential and urban areas is 25 mph.

questions.prompt: The three-second rule is used to:
choices: Maintain a safe following distance
Time how long to wait at a stop sign
Measure how fast you can stop
Decide when to change lanes
questions.explanation: The three-second rule helps you keep a safe gap behind the vehicle ahead under normal conditions.

questions.prompt: In a Rhode Island school zone when children are present, the speed limit is typically:
choices: 20 mph
30 mph
40 mph
10 mph
questions.explanation: School zone speed limits in Rhode Island are commonly reduced to 20 mph when children are present.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or fog, you should adjust your following distance by:
choices: Increasing it beyond three seconds
Keeping it exactly the same
Reducing it to stay closer
Removing it entirely
questions.explanation: Poor conditions lengthen stopping distances, so you should increase your following distance for safety.

questions.prompt: Posted speed limits represent:
choices: The maximum safe speed under ideal conditions
The minimum speed you must drive
A suggested speed you can ignore
The speed for emergencies only
questions.explanation: A speed limit is the maximum legal speed when conditions are ideal; you must slow down when conditions worsen.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic is:
choices: Dangerous because it disrupts the flow of traffic
Always the safest option
Required on all highways
Only a problem at night
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly can be as hazardous as speeding because it interferes with the normal flow of traffic.

questions.prompt: How does speeding affect your ability to stop?
choices: It increases the distance needed to stop
It decreases stopping distance
It has no effect on stopping
It improves your reaction time
questions.explanation: The faster you drive, the longer it takes and the more distance you need to bring your vehicle to a stop.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, slower traffic should generally:
choices: Keep to the right
Stay in the left lane
Drive in the center lane only
Change lanes frequently
questions.explanation: Slower vehicles should keep right, leaving the left lanes open for passing and faster traffic.

questions.prompt: When approaching a sharp curve, you should:
choices: Reduce your speed before entering the curve
Brake hard in the middle of the curve
Accelerate through the curve
Maintain highway speed
questions.explanation: Slow down before a curve so you can maintain control; braking hard mid-curve can cause a skid.

questions.prompt: You must never pass another vehicle when:
choices: A solid yellow line is on your side of the road
A broken yellow line is on your side
You are on a straight, clear road
The vehicle ahead is signaling a turn
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited because it is not safe to do so there.

questions.prompt: When a school bus is stopped with its red lights flashing on an undivided road, you must:
choices: Stop in both directions
Slow down and pass carefully
Pass only on the left
Continue if no children are visible
questions.explanation: On an undivided road, all traffic in both directions must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
You are alongside its front bumper
You feel like it
The other driver signals you
questions.explanation: Returning when you can see the vehicle in your mirror ensures you have left enough room to merge back safely.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal and check your mirrors and blind spots
Only check your rearview mirror
Honk to warn others
Change quickly without signaling
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking mirrors and blind spots ensures no vehicle is in the space you intend to enter.

questions.prompt: It is unsafe and usually illegal to pass:
choices: On a hill or curve where you cannot see far enough ahead
On a long, straight, level road
In a designated passing zone
When the road has a broken center line
questions.explanation: Passing on hills or curves is dangerous because oncoming traffic may be hidden from view.

questions.prompt: On a road with multiple lanes in the same direction, you should pass using:
choices: The left lane
The right shoulder
Any lane you choose
The center turn lane
questions.explanation: Passing should be done in the left lane, while the right lanes are used for normal travel.

questions.prompt: While passing another vehicle, exceeding the speed limit is:
choices: Illegal
Allowed if brief
Required to pass safely
Permitted on highways only
questions.explanation: You must stay within the posted speed limit at all times, even when passing another vehicle.

questions.prompt: A center lane marked with solid and broken yellow lines on both sides is used for:
choices: Left turns from either direction
Passing slower vehicles
Parking
Through traffic only
questions.explanation: A two-way left-turn lane in the center of a road is reserved for vehicles making left turns from either direction.

questions.prompt: When should you move into the proper lane for a turn?
choices: Well before reaching the turn
Right at the intersection
After entering the intersection
Only if traffic is heavy
questions.explanation: Getting into the correct lane early gives you and other drivers time to adjust safely before the turn.

questions.prompt: Rhode Island's Move Over law requires drivers to slow down and, when possible, change lanes for:
choices: Stopped emergency, tow, utility, and maintenance vehicles with flashing lights
Only police cars
Parked passenger cars
School buses only
questions.explanation: The Move Over law protects responders by requiring drivers to slow and move over for stopped vehicles showing flashing lights.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Allow it a full lane and check blind spots carefully
Share the lane side by side
Follow more closely than usual
Pass without signaling
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane and are easy to overlook, so check your blind spots carefully around them.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, so you should:
choices: Avoid lingering alongside them
Stay beside them as long as possible
Follow closely to stay visible
Cut in front right after passing
questions.explanation: Trucks have wide blind spots, so do not linger beside them where the driver cannot see you.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Give them plenty of room
Pass as closely as possible
Honk to make them move over
Speed up to pass quickly with little space
questions.explanation: Bicyclists are vulnerable, so give them a wide berth when passing to keep them safe.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you must:
choices: Yield the right-of-way
Honk to alert them
Proceed as normal
Speed up to clear the area
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog signals a person who is blind, and drivers must always yield to them.

questions.prompt: After passing a large truck, you should not return to its lane until:
choices: You can see the whole front of the truck in your mirror
Your bumper clears the truck
You are halfway past it
The truck driver waves you in
questions.explanation: Wait until you can see the entire front of the truck in your mirror so you do not cut it off.

questions.prompt: Compared to cars, large trucks and buses need:
choices: Much longer distances to stop
Shorter distances to stop
The same stopping distance
No braking distance on highways
questions.explanation: Because of their weight, trucks and buses require far greater distances to come to a complete stop.

questions.prompt: Near a school, bus stop, or playground, you should:
choices: Watch carefully for pedestrians and children
Maintain your normal speed
Use your high beams
Expect children to yield to you
questions.explanation: Children may behave unpredictably, so stay alert and reduce speed near schools, bus stops, and playgrounds.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for drivers 21 and older in Rhode Island?
choices: 0.08 percent
0.05 percent
0.10 percent
0.02 percent
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and over are legally impaired at a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: For drivers under 21 in Rhode Island, a BAC of what level results in penalties?
choices: 0.02 percent or higher
0.08 percent or higher
0.05 percent or higher
0.10 percent or higher
questions.explanation: Rhode Island has a zero-tolerance law, so drivers under 21 face penalties at a BAC of 0.02 percent or more.

questions.prompt: In Rhode Island, using a handheld cell phone while driving is:
choices: Illegal, because it is a hands-free state
Legal for adults
Allowed at red lights
Permitted on highways
questions.explanation: Rhode Island law prohibits holding a phone or wireless device while driving; only hands-free use is allowed.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving in Rhode Island is:
choices: Prohibited
Allowed at low speeds
Allowed for adults only
Permitted when traffic is light
questions.explanation: Texting while driving is banned in Rhode Island because it dangerously takes your eyes off the road.

questions.prompt: By driving on Rhode Island roads, you give implied consent to:
choices: Chemical testing for alcohol or drugs
Random vehicle searches
Annual road tests
Waiving your insurance
questions.explanation: Implied consent means that by driving you have agreed to submit to chemical testing when lawfully requested.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect a driver?
choices: It impairs judgment, reaction time, and coordination
It improves concentration
It has no effect in small amounts
It only affects vision
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reaction time and reduces judgment and coordination, all of which are critical for safe driving.

questions.prompt: Driving while drowsy is dangerous because fatigue:
choices: Affects you much like alcohol
Improves your reflexes
Has no real impact
Only matters on long trips
questions.explanation: Fatigue slows reaction time and impairs judgment in ways similar to alcohol, making drowsy driving hazardous.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a form of distracted driving?
choices: Eating while driving
Keeping both hands on the wheel
Watching the road ahead
Checking your mirrors
questions.explanation: Eating takes your attention and hands away from driving, making it a form of distraction.

questions.prompt: The safest way to handle your phone while driving is to:
choices: Put it away entirely
Hold it only at stoplights
Keep it in your lap
Use it briefly for quick texts
questions.explanation: Putting the phone away removes the temptation and distraction entirely, which is the safest choice.

questions.prompt: In Rhode Island, you must turn on your headlights whenever:
choices: You are using your windshield wipers
It is sunny
You are parked
You are driving below 25 mph
questions.explanation: Rhode Island law requires headlights whenever wipers are in use or visibility is reduced.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer where you want to go
Slam on the brakes
Accelerate hard
Turn the wheel sharply away from the skid
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering gently in the desired direction helps you regain control of a skid.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while moving
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so low beams give you a clearer view.

questions.prompt: The road is often most slippery:
choices: During the first rain after a dry spell
After it has rained for hours
On clear, dry days
When the pavement is brand new
questions.explanation: Oil and dust mix with the first rain to create an especially slippery surface before being washed away.

questions.prompt: When driving in heavy rain or snow, you should:
choices: Slow down and increase your following distance
Maintain your normal speed
Follow closely to see better
Use your high beams
questions.explanation: Reduced traction and visibility in rain or snow require slower speeds and a larger following distance.

questions.prompt: If conditions become too dangerous to drive in, you should:
choices: Pull completely off the road in a safe spot and wait
Stop in your travel lane
Speed up to reach your destination faster
Turn off your lights to save power
questions.explanation: When it is too dangerous to continue, pull fully off the roadway to a safe location and wait for conditions to improve.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning is most likely to occur when:
choices: You drive too fast on a wet road
You drive slowly in the rain
The road is dry
Your tires are new
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning happens when tires ride on a film of water, which is most likely at higher speeds on wet roads.

questions.prompt: To handle bright sun glare or oncoming headlights, you should:
choices: Slow down and look toward the right edge of your lane
Look directly at the light
Speed up to get past it
Close one eye
questions.explanation: Looking toward the right edge of your lane helps you stay on course without being blinded by glare.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
choices: 15 feet
5 feet
25 feet
50 feet
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so it remains accessible to firefighters.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill keeps the car from rolling into traffic if it moves.

questions.prompt: Seat belts in Rhode Island are required for:
choices: The driver and all passengers
Only the driver
Only front-seat occupants
Only children
questions.explanation: Rhode Island law requires the driver and passengers to wear seat belts for their protection.

questions.prompt: Where is it illegal to park?
choices: On a crosswalk or sidewalk
In a marked parking space
In your own driveway
In a public parking lot
questions.explanation: Parking on a crosswalk or sidewalk blocks pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Young children must be secured in a vehicle using:
choices: A proper child safety or booster seat
A regular seat belt only
An adult's lap
No restraint if in the back seat
questions.explanation: Children must ride in an appropriate child safety or booster seat based on their age, height, and weight.

questions.prompt: When you park your vehicle on any hill, you should also:
choices: Set the parking brake
Leave it in neutral
Leave the engine running
Leave the doors unlocked
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake adds protection against the vehicle rolling when parked on a hill.

questions.prompt: You should not park your vehicle:
choices: In front of a driveway
In a legal street space
In a parking garage
Along a curb where parking is allowed
questions.explanation: Parking in front of a driveway blocks access and is not permitted.

questions.prompt: The most effective way to protect yourself in a crash is to:
choices: Wear your seat belt before driving
Drive slowly only
Keep the windows up
Sit in the back seat
questions.explanation: Seat belts are the single most effective device for reducing injuries and deaths in crashes.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a limited instructional permit in Rhode Island?
choices: 16 years old
15 years old
17 years old
18 years old
questions.explanation: A person may apply for a Rhode Island limited instructional permit at age 16 after meeting the requirements.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving must a Rhode Island permit holder complete before advancing?
choices: At least 50 hours, including 10 at night
At least 20 hours total
At least 100 hours, including 30 at night
No specific number
questions.explanation: Before advancing, a permit holder must complete at least 50 hours of supervised practice, including 10 hours at night.

questions.prompt: A Rhode Island permit holder must drive with a licensed adult who is:
choices: At least 21 years old and licensed for at least five years
At least 18 years old
Any licensed driver
A licensed driver over 16
questions.explanation: The supervising driver must be at least 21 and have held a license for at least five years.

questions.prompt: During the first year of a limited provisional license, the driver generally may not drive between:
choices: Midnight and 4:00 a.m.
questions.explanation: Limited provisional license holders are restricted from driving between 1:00 a.m. and 5:00 a.m., with some exceptions.

questions.prompt: In the first year of a limited provisional license, how many passengers under 21 may the driver carry?
choices: Only one, unless they are immediate family
Up to three
No limit
Two, plus an adult
questions.explanation: First-year provisional drivers may carry only one passenger under 21 unless the passengers are immediate family.

questions.prompt: Where must the supervising driver sit while a permit holder is driving?
choices: In the front passenger seat
In the back seat
Anywhere in the vehicle
In a following vehicle
questions.explanation: The licensed supervising driver must be seated in the front passenger seat next to the permit holder.

questions.prompt: To get a limited instructional permit, an applicant under 18 must first:
choices: Complete an approved driver education course
Drive 100 hours alone
Be at least 18 years old
Own a vehicle
questions.explanation: Applicants under 18 must complete an approved driver education course before getting a limited instructional permit.

questions.prompt: You should always carry which documents while driving?
choices: License, registration, and proof of insurance
Only your license
Your birth certificate
Your driver education certificate
questions.explanation: Drivers must carry and present their license, registration, and proof of insurance when requested.

questions.prompt: Refusing a chemical test in Rhode Island results in:
choices: An automatic license suspension
A warning only
A small fine with no other penalty
No consequences
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, refusing a lawful chemical test leads to an automatic suspension of your license.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, you must:
choices: Stop, help the injured, and exchange information
Leave if the damage looks minor
Drive away and report it later only if asked
Move your car and continue driving
questions.explanation: The law requires you to stop, aid the injured, and exchange information; leaving the scene is a serious crime.

questions.prompt: Serious violations such as DUI in Rhode Island can lead to:
choices: License suspension or revocation
A verbal warning only
Points that never affect your license
Lower insurance rates
questions.explanation: Major offenses like driving under the influence can result in suspension or revocation of your driving privilege.

questions.prompt: Leaving the scene of an accident is:
choices: A serious crime
A minor traffic ticket
Allowed if no one is hurt
Permitted if you exchange numbers later
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of a crash, especially one with injuries, is a serious criminal offense.

questions.prompt: Accumulating multiple traffic violations can lead to:
choices: License suspension and higher insurance costs
A reward from the DMV
No consequences at all
Automatic license renewal
questions.explanation: Repeated violations can cause your license to be suspended and typically raise your insurance premiums.

questions.prompt: When a police officer asks for your documents during a stop, you should:
choices: Provide your license, registration, and insurance
Refuse until you call a lawyer
Drive to a police station first
Ignore the request
questions.explanation: You are required to present your license, registration, and proof of insurance when an officer requests them.

questions.prompt: Implied consent in Rhode Island means that by driving you have agreed to:
choices: Submit to a breath or blood test when lawfully requested
Pay all traffic fines in advance
Waive your right to a license
Allow searches of your home
questions.explanation: Implied consent means you have agreed to chemical testing for alcohol or drugs when lawfully asked.

questions.prompt: The best way to avoid traffic penalties is to:
choices: Obey traffic laws and drive courteously
Drive only at night
Avoid highways
Memorize where police patrol
questions.explanation: Following the rules of the road and driving courteously is the surest way to avoid fines and penalties.
